DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MUSLEH'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT SEAN MASON TO: 1. PROVIDE WRITTEN RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE); 2. PROVIDE WRITTEN RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE); 3. PROVIDE WRITTEN RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION (SET ONE), AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS; 4. DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED IN PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS (SET ONE); AND 5. PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS (AS TO DEFENDANT SEAN MASON AND HIS ATTORNEY OF RECORD). THE UNOPPOSED MOTION OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS ALSO GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT SEAN MASON AND HIS FORMER COUNSEL, BRENDAN BRADLEY, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.00. DEFENDANT SEAN MASON SHALL PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT DISCOVERY RESPONSES WITHIN 14 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT SEAN AND/OR HIS FORMER COUNSEL, BRENDAN BRADLEY, SHALL PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF THROUGH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WITHIN 14 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAISL) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM WILLIAM LYNN AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-08-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-01-28 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Sean Mason To: 1. Provide Written Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One); 2. Provide Written Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); 3. Provide Written Responses To Requests For Production (Set One), And Produce Documents; 4. Deem Matters Admitted In Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions (Set One); And 5. Pay Monetary Sanctions (As To Defendant Sean Mason And His Attorney Of Record)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MUSLEH'S Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Sean Mason To: 1. Provide Written Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One); 2. Provide Written Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One); 3. Provide Written Responses To Requests For Production (Set One), And Produce Documents; 4. Deem Matters Admitted In Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions (Set One); And 5. Pay Monetary Sanctions (As To Defendant Sean Mason And His Attorney Of Record)

The unopposed motion of Plaintiff is granted. The request for sanctions is also granted as to Defendant Sean Mason and his former counsel, Brendan Bradley, jointly and severally, in the amount of $1,500.00. Defendant Sean Mason shall provide code-compliant discovery responses within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Defendant Sean and/or his former counsel, Brendan Bradley, shall pay monetary sanctions to Plaintiff through Plaintiff's counsel within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Parties are also reminded to comply with all applicable local rules, including courtesy copy requirements as the Court did not receive a courtesy copy by email. Moving party is additionally reminded that this motion improperly combined various motions with different procedural and substantive requirements and violates the filing fee requirements of Govt. C. sec. 70617(a)

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling by email to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
